description: The x-axis tick labels use wildly inconsistent formatting styles: plain integers ("0", "600"), two decimal places ("200.00"), an abbreviated "K" notation ("0.4K"), a misplaced thousands separator ("8,00"), and a plain four-digit number ("1000"). A reader scanning the axis would struggle to quickly interpret the scale because each label follows a different numerical convention, making comparisons across the axis confusing and error-prone.
